_meta.purpose: Curated SSOT for /endometritis/ topic hub. Condition comparison cards and diagnostic reference rendered as Astro card grids.
_meta.source: Cicinelli 2013/2015, McQueen 2014, Vitagliano 2022, Moreno 2018, Puente 2019, Kitaya 2015, Margulies 2022, Park 2017.
_meta.card_notes: Each card field is an HTML fragment with inline citation hyperlinks to library records. Render with Astro set:html. Inline citations are MANDATORY per editorial policy.

condition_comparison.name: Chronic Endometritis (CE)
condition_comparison.mechanism: Persistent bacterial colonization driving abnormal plasma cell infiltration in the endometrial stroma
condition_comparison.symptoms: Often subclinical. May include brown spotting, atypical bleeding, no dramatic symptoms in most patients.
condition_comparison.treatment: Antibiotic therapy; post-treatment retest biopsy (specialist centers) to confirm resolution
condition_comparison.fertility_link_html: Documented in RPL and implantation failure cohorts. Prior literature cited in Moreno et al. 2018 reports CE in up to 40% of infertile patients.

condition_comparison.name: Endometriosis
condition_comparison.anatomy: Outside the uterus, peritoneal surfaces, ovaries, bowel, bladder, diaphragm
condition_comparison.mechanism: Endometrial-like tissue implants, bleeds cyclically, drives inflammation, adhesions, and organ distortion
condition_comparison.symptoms: Dysmenorrhea, deep dyspareunia, cyclical bowel/bladder symptoms, pelvic pain, infertility
condition_comparison.diagnosis_standard: Laparoscopy with tissue biopsy and histologic confirmation
condition_comparison.treatment: Excision surgery by trained surgeon; adhesion prevention; post-operative cycle monitoring
condition_comparison.fertility_link_html: Affects 25 to 50% of women with infertility per Parasar et al. 2017. See endometriosis guide.

condition_comparison.name: Acute Endometritis
condition_comparison.mechanism: Ascending polymicrobial infection, typically post-delivery, post-D&C, or post-procedure
condition_comparison.symptoms: Fever, uterine tenderness, abnormal discharge, symptomatic and acute
condition_comparison.diagnosis_standard: Clinical presentation and culture; managed in acute care setting
condition_comparison.treatment: Systemic antibiotics; inpatient management if severe
condition_comparison.fertility_link_html: May be sequela risk for CE. Primary concern is acute maternal morbidity, not implantation.

diagnostic_pathway.label: Endometrial biopsy
diagnostic_pathway.detail_html: Minimally invasive office procedure. Sample taken in proliferative phase. Sent for CD138 IHC and standard H&E staining.

diagnostic_pathway.label: CD138 immunohistochemistry
diagnostic_pathway.detail_html: Pathology staining for plasma cells in endometrial stroma. Plasma cells not normally present in stroma. Positive equals CE confirmed. Threshold varies by laboratory protocol, not standardized nationally.

diagnostic_pathway.label: Hysteroscopy
diagnostic_pathway.detail_html: Raises clinical suspicion via characteristic findings: micropolyps, focal hyperemia, stromal edema ("strawberry pattern"). Identifies co-occurring structural pathology. Does NOT confirm CE without biopsy.

diagnostic_pathway.label: Microbial culture
diagnostic_pathway.detail_html: Identifies causative organism and guides antibiotic selection. Complements histologic confirmation. Some organisms not easily culturable. Culture alone insufficient for diagnosis.

diagnostic_pathway.label: Post-treatment retest biopsy
diagnostic_pathway.detail_html: Confirms CE resolution after antibiotic course. Performed in specialist centers and evidence-supported protocols; not yet a universal guideline standard. Guides decision on whether a second treatment course is needed.
